question_text: A car is being pushed with a force of 200 N to the right. Air resistance and friction act on the car with a combined force of 50 N to the left. Calculate the resultant force on the car.
explanation: The resultant force is found by adding forces in the same direction and subtracting forces in opposite directions. The push of 200 N to the right minus the resistance of 50 N to the left gives a resultant of 150 N to the right. This is the net force that will cause the car to accelerate.
